procedures.title: CAT 3516C Quarterly Inspection
procedures.description: Comprehensive quarterly inspection procedure for Caterpillar 3516C marine diesel engines. Includes visual inspection, fluid analysis, belt tension checks, and performance verification.

steps.title: Pre-Inspection Safety Check
steps.instruction: Verify engine is shut down and cooling system pressure is released. Check lockout/tagout is in place. Confirm all personnel are clear of the engine bay.

safetyWarnings.title: Hot Surfaces - Burn Hazard
safetyWarnings.description: Engine components may remain hot for up to 2 hours after shutdown. Do not proceed until surface temperature is below 50°C.

steps.title: Visual Inspection - Engine Block
steps.instruction: Inspect engine block for cracks, leaks, corrosion, or damage. Check all mounting bolts are secure. Look for oil or coolant seepage around gaskets and seals.
steps.photoGuidance: Capture photos from all four sides of the engine block

steps.title: Oil Level and Condition Check
steps.instruction: Remove dipstick and check oil level. Oil should be between MIN and MAX marks. Inspect oil color and consistency. Look for metal particles, water contamination, or fuel dilution.
steps.photoGuidance: Photo of dipstick showing oil level and condition

steps.title: Oil Sample Collection
steps.instruction: Using clean sampling equipment, extract 200ml oil sample from the drain valve. Label sample with equipment ID, date, and operating hours. Submit to lab for analysis.

steps.title: Coolant Level and Condition Check
steps.instruction: Check coolant level in expansion tank. Level should be at COLD FULL mark when engine is cold. Inspect coolant color (should be bright green/red). Check for contamination or oil presence.

safetyWarnings.title: Pressurized System
safetyWarnings.description: Never remove radiator cap when engine is hot. System pressure can cause severe burns.

steps.title: Belt Tension Inspection
steps.instruction: Check all drive belts for proper tension, cracks, fraying, or glazing. Using belt tension gauge, verify tension is within specification: 120-140 Hz for alternator belt, 100-120 Hz for water pump belt.

steps.title: Air Filter Inspection
steps.instruction: Remove air filter element and inspect for dirt accumulation, damage, or oil contamination. Check filter housing for cracks or loose connections. Measure pressure drop across filter if gauge is installed.
steps.photoGuidance: Photo showing both sides of air filter element

steps.title: Fuel Filter Inspection
steps.instruction: Check fuel filters for water accumulation in sight glass. Drain water separator if water is present. Inspect fuel lines for leaks, cracks, or chafing.

steps.title: Battery Inspection
steps.instruction: Inspect battery terminals for corrosion. Check battery hold-down brackets are secure. Measure battery voltage with multimeter (should be 12.6V or higher when fully charged). Check electrolyte level if applicable.

steps.title: Hose and Clamp Inspection
steps.instruction: Inspect all coolant hoses, fuel hoses, and air hoses for cracks, bulges, softness, or deterioration. Check all hose clamps are tight. Replace any suspect hoses.

steps.title: Exhaust System Inspection
steps.instruction: Check exhaust manifold and piping for cracks or leaks. Inspect exhaust insulation for damage. Look for soot deposits indicating exhaust leaks.

safetyWarnings.title: Hot Exhaust Components
safetyWarnings.description: Exhaust components retain heat. Ensure adequate cooling time before inspection.

steps.title: Turbocharger Inspection
steps.instruction: Check turbocharger mounting bolts are secure. Inspect for oil leaks around seals. Manually spin compressor wheel to check for binding or excessive play. Listen for unusual bearing noise.

steps.title: Electrical Connections Check
steps.instruction: Inspect all electrical connectors, wiring harnesses, and grounds. Check for loose connections, corrosion, chafing, or damaged insulation. Verify all connections are clean and tight.

steps.title: Instrument Panel Functional Test
steps.instruction: Turn ignition to ON position (do not start engine). Verify all gauges and warning lights illuminate. Check all displays are functioning. Test alarm functions if equipped.

steps.title: Engine Fluid Top-Off
steps.instruction: If any fluid levels are low, top off with approved fluids. Engine oil: CAT DEO 15W-40. Coolant: 50/50 ethylene glycol mix. Record quantities added.

steps.title: Start Engine and Initial Running Check
steps.instruction: Start engine and let idle for 2 minutes. Monitor oil pressure (should reach 40 PSI within 15 seconds). Check coolant temperature rises normally. Listen for unusual noises or vibrations.

safetyWarnings.title: Moving Parts - Keep Clear
safetyWarnings.description: Keep hands, tools, and clothing away from moving engine components while running.

steps.title: Load Test and Parameter Recording
steps.instruction: Increase engine load to 75% rated power. Record parameters after 10 minutes: oil pressure, coolant temp, oil temp, exhaust temp, boost pressure, RPM. Compare to baseline values.
steps.photoGuidance: Photo of gauge panel showing all parameters

steps.title: Post-Run Leak Check
steps.instruction: After 10 minutes of running, shut down engine. Immediately inspect for any new leaks that may appear under operating temperature and pressure. Check oil, coolant, fuel, and exhaust systems.

steps.title: Documentation and Sign-Off
steps.instruction: Complete inspection checklist. Record all findings, measurements, and actions taken. Note any issues requiring follow-up. Update equipment maintenance log with inspection date and next due date.

safetyRequirements.title: Lockout/Tagout Required
safetyRequirements.description: Engine must be locked out and tagged before beginning maintenance. Verify zero energy state.

procedures.title: Emergency Generator Weekly Load Test
procedures.description: Weekly load bank test procedure for emergency diesel generator to verify operational readiness and performance under load conditions.

steps.title: Pre-Test Safety Brief
steps.instruction: Verify area is clear of personnel. Post warning signs at generator room entrance. Confirm load bank is properly connected and ventilation is adequate.

safetyWarnings.title: High Noise Level
safetyWarnings.description: Generator produces noise levels exceeding 85 dB. Hearing protection is mandatory.

steps.title: Visual Pre-Start Inspection
steps.instruction: Check oil level, coolant level, fuel level. Inspect for leaks, loose connections, or damaged components. Verify battery connections are clean and tight.

steps.title: Start Generator - No Load
steps.instruction: Start generator and allow to warm up for 5 minutes at no load. Monitor oil pressure, coolant temperature, frequency, and voltage. Verify all parameters are within normal range.

steps.title: Apply 25% Load
steps.instruction: Connect load bank and apply 25% rated load (137.5 kW). Run for 5 minutes. Record voltage, frequency, current, oil pressure, coolant temp, exhaust temp.

steps.title: Apply 50% Load
steps.instruction: Increase load to 50% rated (275 kW). Run for 10 minutes. Record all parameters. Check for abnormal noise, vibration, or smoke.

steps.title: Apply 75% Load
steps.instruction: Increase load to 75% rated (412.5 kW). Run for 10 minutes. Record all parameters. Verify generator is stable under sustained load.

steps.title: Return to No Load
steps.instruction: Reduce load to zero in 25% increments, allowing 2 minutes at each step. Monitor parameters during unloading.

steps.title: Cool Down and Shutdown
steps.instruction: Run generator at no load for 5 minutes to cool down. Monitor coolant temperature drops below 80°C. Shut down generator.

steps.title: Post-Test Inspection
steps.instruction: Inspect for leaks or abnormalities that appeared during test. Check oil level has not dropped. Verify coolant level is stable.

safetyWarnings.title: Hot Surfaces
safetyWarnings.description: Engine and exhaust components are hot. Allow cooling before touching.

steps.title: Document Results
steps.instruction: Complete load test report. Record all measurements, observations, and any deficiencies found. Note next test due date (7 days from today).
